sections.title: Feeding him something he recently ate

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Uuummmpf...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you hear me?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Owww... paaaain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't move, don't move!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll be okay. You'll be fine.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Help me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will, just stay still.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you hear me?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Just about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just don't worry.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: You think things will turn out okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, probably not, to be honest.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I thought so. I'm doomed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to have to go soon.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh no, please don't leave me alone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I have work to do.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I don't think I will survive much longer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's quite dark down here.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I hadn't noticed. Who knows what danger lurks in the shadows. I'm so scared.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you hear that noise?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: What!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought I heard something...

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh no, they're coming to get us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, it's okay. I must have been imagining things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're looking better now.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: You're lying. I look dreadful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, honest.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Really? In what way?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look dead any more, for a start.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Well it's a start, I suppose. Doesn't sound too promising.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you look and sound more lively.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: That's promising. Perhaps I will make it out of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long have you been down here?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I don't know, probably days. I just can't remember.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's quite terrible.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This place gives me the creeps!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Me too. I'm so scared. Please don't leave me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I shall have to soon.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Feeling positive?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you looking forward to getting out?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Definitely. A good wash, fresh clothes, a nice meal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just take it a step at a time.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Sorry, I was getting a bit over-excited there, I think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the spirit!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Thank you, your help really is appreciated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you look so sad?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Wouldn't you if you were about to die?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I imagine so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cheer up, it may never happen!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: True, I may never get out of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Always so negative. It's not good for you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Those are some deep cuts on your legs.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Cuts? I hadn't noticed. Where?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There, just above your shin.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh no, oh no, oh no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stop panicking!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Easy for you to say!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I feel a bit ill.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Ill? You can't feel ill, I'm depending on you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't help being ill.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh dear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem like a nice guy.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Ah, thanks. That's a really nice thing to say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought I had better say it before it's too late.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh, I see. I understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just being honest.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Thank you. Right back at ya!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't believe how many suqahs are on this island.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: You're right. I really should never have come to this island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps you're right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you think you should give up being an adventurer?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: What makes you say that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, erm... you know.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: No, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't seem to be doing too well at the moment.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Thanks for the support, friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When we get out of here I'll buy you a drink!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Naturally.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: That's nice, what kind?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whatever and wherever you want - my treat.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: That's really appreciated. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something cheap. Although it's my pleasure, helping you is costing me.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Okay. Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hope you don't mind me saying, but you smell a bit.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: No it's not, this is so embarrassing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It'll be a while before we get out.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh no. I don't think I can stand much more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you have to. You just have to.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Air. I wish I could breathe fresh air.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm very impressed you managed to get into this cave.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I must admit it was hard to crawl this far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would have given up personally.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Really? I was determined to survive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Congratulations on not dying!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh. Erm. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good job you didn't find more suqah down here.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I suppose so. But now that I think about it, I don't know why there are none down here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder why nothing lives down here.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I haven't really thought about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's probably not very easy to survive down here.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Good point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I trust you usually look smarter.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Yes, I am a bit scruffy at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just a bit.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not to worry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a bit damp in here.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Is it? I suppose so. Why do you say that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just concerned that it will make your condition worse.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: It wouldn't surprise me. I don't seem to be having much luck recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Spider!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Arrghh! Where?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just kidding.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh, you scared me to death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll survive this easily.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I'm not so sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Come on. Think positive!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: What's the point?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Think of all the places you can visit when you get out!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: That's true.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Such possibilities and adventures!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Yes. I can't wait!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because otherwise you'll fail.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Failure...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. So you must think positive.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I'll try, maybe when I feel a bit stronger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had better be going now.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh no please don't go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I... I... need you to help me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you going to do when you get out of here?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Well I don't know. What should I do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's up to you. You could travel with me!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: You'd do that for me? That sounds marvellous! I will look forward to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I were you I'd stay indoors.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: You have a good point there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any chance you could pay me for this?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Perhaps. Oh, but I don't think I have any money left on me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shame.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's very cold down here.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Yes. Could you perhaps give me a blanket?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry. I don't have any on me.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I'll probably be okay. Thanks, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a good thing you have me to look after you.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: That is true.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I rule!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: You ought to be careful with that attitude.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't have to worry about anything because I'm the best!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: We'll see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not that I'm bragging or anything.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: That's okay. I think you deserve it a bit for saving my life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would you do if I wasn't here?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Probably be dead I suppose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quite the coincidence that I found you!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Yes, I think I've been very lucky.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That you have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't know how long you've been down here?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: No. Could have been days. I don't remember much after stumbling down the ladder: just crawling.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's quite scary.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not long now and you'll be back on your feet!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh, my friend, you really think so?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That kind of depends.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: On what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On whether we have to amputate your legs or not.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Amputate! You can't be serious?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ooops, erm, no I'm sure it won't come to that.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh no. Why can't I die peacefully?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On whether you mind me helping you further.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I'm so glad to have your help. I would never turn it down!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am glad.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think there is anyone else down here?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Depends on what the person is like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, hopefully someone nice.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh I do hope so!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've got quite the collection of bruises there!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I know. It hurts to move.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They will mend in time.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Time is something I don't have much of.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Since when did you only have one leg?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: One leg! What the?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hahaha.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just kidding.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: So mean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm tired, I think I'll take a nap.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh, no reason. Just thought you would help me out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think this cave is stable?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I'm not too sure. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, hence why I asked you.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh. I hope it's safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're sounding much better.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: My voice, you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, much more positive. Which is great.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you probably want to sort out the bad breath.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll let you off. But just this once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you need anything, just let me know.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have a big appetite.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I am hungry. Sorry for eating all your food.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll let you off I suppose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's sing a song!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Is it really the time to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Half a pound of tuppenny rice, half a pound of treacle.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the way the money goes. Pop goes the weasel!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Please go away...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's quite cosy in here.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: That's a strange thing to say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't like it?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: No, not really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's no pleasing you, is there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about a joke!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Please, I don't...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does the queen bee get around her hive?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Okay, go on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's throne.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: That was bad.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This would probably make a nice home.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: You think?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, do you?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Not really, I think I'll stick to living ABOVE ground.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We could do with something for you to sit on.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I just thought it would be a bit more civil.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: You're a bit weird.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you smell gas?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Gas? Why would you smell gas?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just thought I could, you know... mines often have poisonous gases.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Please, let's talk about something else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me tell you about myself!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I started off my life as a simple adventurer, battling goblins, picking up coins on the floor...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I remember the day when I was excited to find a thousand coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now it is nothing!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Sorry to stop you, but are you going to help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a fan of stories, then? Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want to talk about?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: I don't know, I don't think I feel like talking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your loss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it okay if I talk to you?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: No, I think I'd rather be left alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me a bit about yourself.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, you.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Well, I grew up in Ardougne. Erm, I quite enjoy Fishing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fishing?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds a bit boring to me.

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Don't knock it till you've tried it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fishing!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I like fishing too!

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Perhaps we can fish together one day?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd love to!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you hear me?

lines.speaker: Fallen Man
lines.text: Just about.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm so pleased you're getting better! You must stay still though. You're not fit to walk yet.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Where am I? Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My name's [player name]. You're on Lunar Isle, in a cave.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I'm Cyrisus. Oh yes, I remember, I was on the island and...

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Suqahs! There were suqah monsters!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: They attacked! I ran and ran. I crawled down here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay, it's okay. You're safe now.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I am?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I was so afraid.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Oh my head hurts. And my legs. I'm so tired.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Please, just leave me, I'm a waste of effort, my body is in ruin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, just trust me, I'll see that you get out of here. Don't give up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there. How are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Still painful.

sections.title: Using edible items on him after learning his name
Feeding him something he recently ate after learning his name
Using food on him after reaching food threshold

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There we are.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Thank you [player name]. I would surely be dead without you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry. So what were you doing on this island?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Exploring. I greatly enjoy travelling the world and taking in all the experiences available.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can relate to that, and Lunar Isle is definitely worth a visit. Where else have you been?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Well, I've travelled from the frozen wastes in the Wilderness to the pyramids of the Kharidian Desert. I've lived with the monkeys of Ape Atoll and dined with the TzHaar under the volcano of Karamja.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Impressive. You must have a great many stories and mastered loads of skills.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: You could say that.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I could smith you full rune, farm spirit trees and still have time to cook you a summer pie for tea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow. No need to say more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why have you had such difficulty with the suqah? Surely you're a skilled warrior?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Err, well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must have mastered all forms of combat?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: No. Not really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I... erm...

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Please, let's leave that line of conversation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay. If you wish. So, what now?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I still don't feel fit enough to go outside. I could also do with some armour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I could loan you some armour?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Oh, there's really no need to worry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Trust, me, there's no need. Please, just go to the bank on this island, I have a friend working there. Just tell him I sent you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what does he look like? What's his name?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Trust me: when you see him, you'll know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okaaay.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Oh, and if you want, you can contact me with the NPC Contact spell in the Lunar Spellbook. It might save you running back and forth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're in need of some armour?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: It would be good, yes. You should be able to talk to my friend in the bank on this island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Hi! Managed to get my armour yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I had best visit the bank and see what I can get.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Well, I would appreciate it if you could hurry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I'm getting a bit cold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there, this may sound a bit random, but do you know Cyrisus?

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: That I do. But I must say you seem quite familiar too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do?

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Why! You're that insolent fool who insulted my navigational skills!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What!

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Accusing me of navigating in circles!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait a minute! Now I recognise you; you're the navigator on the ship that first took me to Lunar Isle! The first time I visited this place your navigation was useless!

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: How dare you! It was a jinx.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, if it wasn't for me you would never have reached this place.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Oh, behold the great adventurer [player name]. How the world would crumble without her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, the wit.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Better than yours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, no it isn't.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Er. Yes it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er. No, it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stop. Let's not do that again. So, why are you working here now?

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Why, you have a problem with it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no. It's just that the job of banker doesn't seem quite as... 'exotic' as 'pirate'. Seems like a step-down to me.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: How dare you! I'll have you know that the job of a banker is far more exciting than that of a pirate!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, yeah, whatever you say mate.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: It is! And it's an honest living.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: I was tired of looting and hunting for treasure. It was time to turn my life around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What a heart touching story.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Don't patronise me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, as I was saying, do you know Cyrisus?

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, he's down in the caves at the north of this island, and I need your help.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Listening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get some items out of his bank account. Is that possible?

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: I suppose. Knock yourself out.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: I suppose you had better take this chest to carry his stuff. Don't want you losing any of it.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Ah, I would if you had any space.

sections.title: Getting a new chest after losing it

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. Could I access Cyrisus's bank account again.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Okay. You best help him quickly or else I'll be after you!

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Ah, you can't unless you have the chest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm afraid I have lost it.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Well, I don't suppose it would be a problem to give you another. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Looking in the chest while it's empty
Reopening his bank with the chest in your inventory

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. Could I access Cyrisus's bank account again.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Okay. You best help him quickly or else I'll be after you!

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Ah, you can't unless you have the chest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I think I left it in my bank account.

sections.title: Showing him gear through NPC Contact

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Hi! Managed to get my armour yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have got a chest and filled it with some items.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Superb! What have you got?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Robin hood hat

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: It doesn't particularly impress me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Karil's leathertop

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: No comment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Black d'hide chaps

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Not too sure about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ranger boots

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: That's spot-on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Magic shortbow

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Hmmm, I'd rather something else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad you know what you want.

sections.title: Showing him the correct gear through NPC Contract

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Hi! Managed to get my armour yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have got a chest and filled it with some items.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Superb! What have you got?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dragon med helm

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Perfect!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahrim's robetop

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Oh, good choice!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahrim's robeskirt

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Very good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ranger boots

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: That's spot-on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Abyssal whip

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Superb!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad you know what you want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I've been to see 'Bird's-Eye' Jack, and I've got a chest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've also filled it with some armour; what do you think?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Okay, I'll try it on. No peeking!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I won't. I'll close my eyes.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Still changing.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Hmmm....

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I've put the clothes back in the chest, they're not too bad.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: But not too good either. I need a different combination.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm confused, any tips?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I can't stand the item of headgear you've chosen - get rid of that straight away!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: What's with the body armour? Totally not my style!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I'd much rather have something else protecting my legs.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: The footwear has to go. I can't rely on them to help me.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I'm not all that good at using the type of weapon you offered. Please bring something else.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Hmmm....

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Perfect! I couldn't ask for a better set-up!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excellent!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looking good in your kit!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Perfect! I feel healthy, I'm positively charged and I'm dressed like a war machine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excellent!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You certainly look the part!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Why thanks, I feel it too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, Cyrisus. Please tell me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you have such difficulty against the suqahs?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Ah, well. Best I tell you. The thing is... the thing that I should say... the problem I have, for which you should know...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just tell me.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I'm afraid of combat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I've tried so hard over the years, but I simply cannot gather the courage to fight monsters.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I've learnt the techniques, I've got the equipment. I just get so afraid. My legs turn to jelly, I'm dripping with sweat. All I can do is run.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now that surprises me.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I know it seems bad, but honestly, I've tried. It was part of the reason I came to Lunar Isle to see the Moon Clan. I trust you've gone through the ceremony to visit the Dreamland?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have indeed. A very strange day I must say, but very valuable! Especially gaining the Lunar spells.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Yes, so I came to the Moon Clan again to see if they could help me visit my dreams again and conquer my fear of combat. But then I came across the suqah.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Say, would you come with me to the Oneiromancer? Perhaps you can give me some support or guidance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I suppose. Can't see why not.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Excellent! Please, you go on ahead, I'll make my way there myself. I'm not walking out of this cave past those suqahs again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ready?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Of course! You just go speak with the Oneiromancer and I'll be right with you, faster than you'd think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: How's Cyrisus? Where is he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's fine now. Back to full health.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Good, I'm pleased.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Don't think that this means I like you now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look at my face.

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Yes? What about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does it look like I care?

lines.speaker: Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, there you go.

sections.title: Talking to Cyrisus next to the Oneiromancer first

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Cyrisus.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Hi [player name]. I must admit to being a bit nervous. Could you talk to the Oneiromancer? I want to get this out of the way quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hello there. What do you want to talk about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still making the potion. Can you tell me what ingredients it takes again?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Water, an astral rune and goutweed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Getting a new dream vial if lost

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hello there. What do you want to talk about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any chance you have a vial you can give me?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: And the magic word is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm. Lunar?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Oh dear, of course not!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry. PLEASE!

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Now that wasn't difficult, was it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently so. I don't have any free space to take a vial. I'll be back in a second.

sections.title: Talking to Cyrisus next to the brazier

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I have the Potion of Shared Dreaming; now what?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Best you light the brazier with a tinderbox first, then give me the potion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, let's go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Let me know when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so we need to make the Potion of Shared Dreaming.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Yes. Water, goutweed and one astral rune all in that...

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Wait, where did you put the vial?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oops.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Ask the Oneiromancer nicely, I'm sure she will have another.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so we need to make the Potion of Shared Dreaming.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Yes. Water, goutweed and one astral rune all in that tiny vial.

sections.title: Talking to the Oneiromancer after making the potion

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hello there. What do you want to talk about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well so far, we have the Potion of Shared Dreaming.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We just need to visit Cyrisus's dreams and come to a solution.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: I like your spirit!

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hello there. What do you want to talk about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Oh hello [player name]! It has been a while.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: What brings you here again? Creating more astral runes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not at the moment. I've come on behalf of a friend. You will probably have met him - his name is Cyrisus.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Oh yes, I know him. A bright man. What is it about him, my dear?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I...

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Oh my.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Cyrisus. It's been a while. Your kind friend here was just talking about you.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Good. We were hoping you could help us.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Yes? I can try.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I need to get over my fear of combat. Surely I can sort that out in the Dreamland?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Of course. With the Potion of Shared Dreaming. It'll send you both into the Dreamland, and sort out your biggest issue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds simple.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: In most ways, yes. However, it will be dangerous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: To get you into the dream together, the potion works by binding your subconsciousnesses together. The brazier has its own intelligence in interpreting your subconscious so it will understand your intentions when

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: placing you in the dream.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Okay, I shall just take your word for that bit, but I still can't see the dangerous part...

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: With your minds melded as one, you need each other to survive. If one of you dies, you both die for real. Unlike before, you will die just as if you were killed by a monster in the wild.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I'm not so sure about this anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry Cyrisus, we can do this. Oneiromancer, how do we make the potion?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Simple. I shall give you a vial, within which you should place water followed by one ground astral rune and goutweed. Then just use it on the brazier. Oh! You'll have to light it first, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Good luck!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Wait. Do we need to wear the clothes we had to wear to go to the dreamland before.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Oh no, no need. They were simply ceremonial clothes, you can wear whatever you want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: See you at the brazier!

sections.title: Talking to the Oneiromancer after entering the dream

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hello there. What do you want to talk about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not bad. We've been into the dream, but we are yet to conquer it.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Very good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're probably going to need more of that potion.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Perhaps Cyrisus can help you out there, he's a resourceful chap.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to go into the dream again.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Do we have to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: It looks like there is a little potion left in the bottle.

sections.title: Trying to re-enter with the brazier unlit

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to go into the dream again.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: That may be the case, but you need to light the brazier with a tinderbox first.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Fight to give me courage.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Hope you're okay, [player name]

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: You shall never get me!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Have vengeance!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Oh, I can't attack. I'm too afraid!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I'm building up my courage. I'll attack soon.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Fight to give me courage.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I don't know if I can do this.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Take this!

lines.speaker: The Inadequacy
lines.text: Graaaaarrr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, now, now! Cyrisus! Get it!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Okay! Take this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You did it!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I did! Yes! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I knew you'd do it.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: What now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Back to reality, I suppose, and on to the Oneiromancer!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: See you there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrghhh!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on Gielinor is that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cyrisus?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cyrisus...

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: That lectern is rather pretty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cyrisus.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Turn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Around.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Arghh!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't ask me, it's your dream!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must really be afraid of combat!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean 'why'? This huge creature must symbolise your fears!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I'm guessing we're going to be fighting my fears.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cyrisus. Let's do this!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I'm still scared. Show me how you fight, and I should gain courage.

sections.title: Talking to Cyrisus after conquering his fears

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You did it!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: No, WE did it. Thank you [player name]. It really is appreciated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, no worries, it was fun.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Well I'm not sure I enjoyed almost getting killed, but yes, it was good.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hello there. What do you want to talk about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We did it!

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You did?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Yes, I've conquered my fear of combat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We beat those monsters well and good, didn't we?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: We sure did! I was very afraid to start off with, but once I saw how you dealt with combat I felt sure I could do it too.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Oh, I'm pleased. Congratulations.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Thank you [player name]. I really couldn't have done it without your help. Please, let me pass some of my knowledge onto you.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Ah, sorry but could you do me a favour and clear some inventory space first?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, in what way?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: On my last visit to this island I spent a good while with the Moon Clan. It seems it isn't all that impossible to instantly transfer knowledge to one another.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I shall just use lunar magic to pass some information onto you.

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: Just keep your mind blank, relax and hold still.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ooooh. That was a weird sensation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must admit, I do feel enlightened already. Thank you!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: My pleasure. And I best be off, I think I am going to put my courage to the test! I have a new goal to reach - the ultimate warrior!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Be careful!

lines.speaker: Cyrisus
lines.text: I will. I'll see you around, I'm sure!
